rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular oxytrifluoromethylation of N-tethered alkenyl alcohols using Langlois reagent (CF3SO2Na) to deliver 2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethyl)morpholines
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na (Langlois reagent) generates CF3SO2 radical that extrudes SO2 to release CF3 radical; a second anodic SET oxidizes the alkyl radical adduct to a carbocation that is trapped intramolecularly by the tethered alcohol

key_intermediates.name: CF3SO2 radical (from anodic SET on CF3SO2Na)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2Na by SET to give CF3SO2 radical which loses SO2 to form CF3 radical; CF3 radical adds regioselectively to the terminal alkene to form a secondary C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; the tethered hydroxyl performs intramolecular nucleophilic addition with loss of H+ to give the morpholine, while cathodic reduction of H+/H2O liberates H2.

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction in the absence of electric current (Table 1, entry 10), confirming electrochemical initiation

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Langlois reagent vs substrate 1a
probe_reagents.observation: CF3SO2Na shows irreversible anodic oxidation at 1.48 V (vs Ag/AgCl), substantially lower than substrate 1a at 2.07 V, indicating selective oxidation of Langlois reagent at the anode

notes: Mechanism proposed on the basis of control experiments (no current = no reaction), CV data, and analogy to prior reports; no radical trap (e.g., TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT studies are reported. Method also extends to a 7-membered benzoxazepine from an alken-7-ol substrate (Scheme 3).
